So if you have pictures and check-ins and things like that, they're all stored in the cloud to that thread. Alright, now the responsibilities of the coach. You're going to want to put the client into a Monday, Wednesday, Friday, or Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday group, which you will see in the sheet. Joey's going to break down exactly how to use the tracking sheet, but you're going to put clients in one of those two categories just like you would normally have workouts on Monday, Wednesday, Friday, Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday. You're going to be purposely checking in with clients on Monday, Wednesday, Friday, or Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday. It decreases your per day workload and it spreads it out throughout the week. Alright, next you're going to respond to either of those groups on those days when it is that day. Now, for that day, so Monday would be day one or Tuesday would be day one depending on the group, you're going to want to ask questions that are fitness related. An example of that might be like, how are your workouts going? How was your walk today? Did you get outside? Because it was bad today. A million ways you can ask the same question, right? But it's all related to the fitness activity that is related to where they're at right now, which is on the client tracking log. So it makes it very easy. Day two is going to be your nutrition related questions. So that might be Wednesday or Thursday depending on the group. Alright, so nutrition related question would be like, how are you doing with your food? Do you like the foods you're having right now? Are you struggling? Are you hungry? Are you related to the nutrition components? Alright, day three, which would be Friday or Saturday depending on the group. How are you sleeping? How's your energy levels? Those are great questions to lead in because you can build a little bit of a right and people care a lot to know that other people care about them. Now, on the days that you are not messaging them back with a lengthy response or a question, excuse me, you want to always acknowledge communication. Alright, now in those that did not. Very quick. They want to know that someone is watching. That is why they're paying. They're paying for someone to care. They're paying for someone to pay attention. And that is the exchange. And that is what we're paying for. And we can do this in minutes rather than in hours. Now client-wise, they just need to send a picture of the scale. And the reason we did that is because whenever you try and do Google Sheets and things like that, sometimes the length that they fill it out is lower. And then you'd also have to have an entirely separate huge thing of Google Sheets for every single customer that you'd have to track, which could be a big pain. So it also allows them to not cheat and lie on the weight that they're doing and just type in an update and timestamp it so you can make sure that it's a fresh number on the scale. And this way it creates a habit for them. Alright, so for those reasons, we do the picture of the scale. And we honestly don't need to know the actual weight. We just need to make sure that we know the average when they come in for the bi-weekly check-in, which is what we will cover during the bi-weekly check-in. And so yeah, and that's the responsibility that they have beyond actually just doing the thing. So for them, this is pretty easy. All they have to do is just follow the instructions and send a picture every day. Makes fulfillment for them very easy. So you'll be able to knock these out fairly quickly with products, etc. It also, from a communication standpoint, it's much more difficult to leave someone who you're seeing in person already. But I would recommend highly leveraging the fact that we are in person. For those of you who are like, well, why would someone want to drive 35 minutes to come? It's like, well, they drive to the gym four times a week for 35 minutes there and back, so they're still saving tons of time. And this way, it's important for you to gauge how they're feeling in person, where you can receive a lot more data just by being in the same room as someone. Why? It increases engagement, retention, and ease of communication, especially when communicating the changes of programming, because there's some things that you can just demonstrate and teach better in person, with your hands and pulling the sheet over and explaining it, things like that. And then it keeps them in this cadence of twice per month. And the reason we do twice per month is because if you look at every weight loss clinic franchise, et cetera, that is typically the cadence that they've done. And I would bet dollars to donuts that they have tested, which is the best cadence to minimally be invasive to the client, but also maximally effective. All right.
